events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are given a scenario: ABS-to-PVC glued with wrong cement. This is a material transition that is typically not allowed unless using a specific transition cement approved for both materials. Standard practice for ABS to PVC transitions requires a mechanical coupling or an approved transition cement. The IPC and manufacturer listings would specify that you cannot use standard cement for dissimilar plastics. Since they used the wrong cement, that would be non-compliant and defective. It's at rough-complete stage, so can still be fixed. Decision: fail. Risk: high (because if left uncorrected, the joint may fail, leading to leaks). Work_stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent_complete: likely 100% of this joint, but overall project? They said rough-complete, so maybe around 80%? But we are only assessing this component. I'll estimate 80% overall? The task says "percent_complete is your numeric estimate of overall progress." So maybe 80% for the plumbing rough-in? I'll put 80. Value: 0 (no numeric reading). Confidence: 0.95. Findings: ["ABS-to-PVC transition glued with wrong cement; not approved for this material combination per manufacturer and IPC"]. Actions: ["Replace the joint with an approved transition fitting or mechanical coupling using proper cement/primer as per manufacturer instructions."]. Rationale: "Using incorrect cement for ABS-to-PVC transition violates IPC and manufacturer listings, creating a high risk of joint failure." References: ["IPC Section 303.3", "Manufacturer instructions for cement compatibility"]. Note: IPC 303.3 says materials shall be installed in accordance with the manufacturer's instructions. And 705 for joints? Actually, for plastic pipe, IPC references ASTM standards. So I'll reference IPC 303.3 and mfr instructions.

Output JSON only.
